programmes.show_description: Richie Anderson is live from Salford with your Monday Motivation to kick-start a brand new week, plus Secret Star and This Week in Songs.

programmes.show_name: Alternative Sounds of the 90s with Dermot O'Leary
programmes.show_description: Dermot picks his favourite 90s albums which include The Color and The Shape, Urban Hymns and Automatic For The People. Plus, Kylie picks her favourite 90s band for us.
It's Early Breakfast with Owain Wyn Evans, featuring Step Back in Time and Airdrum Anthems -  Owain is asking about your first pets and advice on being a first time dog owner!
OJ has your morning sorted with Sinitta and music to get you up and out!
Making your mid-mornings memorable, Gary's bringing big tunes, big names and big laughs!
Tony lights up bank holiday Monday with a live Soulful celebration of the very best music, including Luther Vandross, Candi Staton, Beverley Knight, George Benson & Ce Ce Peniston.
Mark Goodier presents his essential selection of the finest 'billion streamers' from the past six decades of rock and pop.
Vernon Kay presents highlights of the performances from this year's Piano Room Month, accompanied by the BBC Concert Orchestra in the BBC's Maida Vale studios.
Jo Whiley introduces Pulp performing in front of a small audience at the BBC Radio Theatre.

programmes.show_name: The Blues Show with Cerys Matthews
programmes.show_description: Grammy-nominated Malian singer-songwriter, guitarist, and actress, Fatoumata Diawara pops in to chat about her new music and to share her favourite blues records and influences.

programmes.show_name: The Good Groove with DJ Spoony
programmes.show_description: Feel-good dancefloor favourites and soul gems, all five minutes in duration and longer to make you groove! Super luxurious tuneage to round up your bank holiday weekend!
Connor Phillips is live through the night from the Salford Riviera with Midnight Mastermind, Wish You Were There and the first Midnight Caller of the week.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 90s with Fearne Cotton
programmes.show_description: Yeehaw! Fearne's got an hour of tunes from the likes of Shania Twain, Billy Ray Cyrus, LeAnn Rimes, Dolly Parton, Rednex, and so much more!
It's Early Breakfast with Owain Wyn Evans with Step Back in Time and Airdrum Anthems! It's coffee and cake day. If you could sit down and have a cuppa with ANYONE who would it be?
Gary Davies has your morning sorted with Delta Goodrem and music to get you up and out!
Mark Goodier sits in and is joined by singer, actor and Radio 2 Love Songs presenter Michael Ball for this week's Tracks Of My Years.
Jeremy and guests discuss the news headlines and talk to the people making them.
Trevor is bringing you some of the best music from across the decades to get you through the afternoon.
Sara gets you through teatime with plenty of giggles and some fab music. Plus Totally Teavoted, the SARA Awards, Lost Four Five Words and Hatched, Matched and Dispatched.
Sara plays the biggest and best tunes to get you up dancing!
Jo brings her Shiny Happy Playlist, 30 minutes of feel-good music from 7-7.30pm to soundtrack listeners' weekday evening.
Jo chats to movie critic James King about the latest films you won't want to miss - and this week, he's chatted to Keanu Reeves and Cameron Diaz!

programmes.show_name: The Jazz Show with Jamie Cullum
programmes.show_description: Jamie has classics from New Orleans legend Sweet Emma Barrett and pianist Duke Pearson, new releases from Jeff Goldblum and Nubiyan Twist, plus BBC Introducing artist Eve Buckley.

programmes.show_name: The Good Groove with DJ Spoony
programmes.show_description: Feel-good tunes to make you groove! Plus, Maverick Sabre chooses the tracks that take him back to the dance floors of his youth in Rewind Selector.
